source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the diagram, it is known that the equilateral triangle $\triangle ABC$ has a side length of $12$, $D$ is a moving point on $AB$, a line is drawn through $D$ such that $DE \perp BC$ at point $E$, a line is drawn through $E$ such that $EF \perp AC$ at point $F$, and a line is drawn through $F$ such that $FG \perp AB$ at point $G$. When $G$ coincides with $D$, what is the length of $AD$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Let AD=x, \\
since $\triangle ABC$ is an equilateral triangle, \\
therefore $\angle A = \angle B = \angle C = 60^\circ$, \\
since DE$\perp$BC at point E, EF$\perp$AC at point F, FG$\perp$AB at point G, \\
therefore $\angle BDF = \angle DEB = \angle EFC = 90^\circ$, \\
therefore AF=2x, \\
therefore CF=12-2x, \\
therefore CE=2CF=24-4x, \\
therefore BE=12-CE=4x-12, \\
therefore BD=2BE=8x-24, \\
since AD+BD=AB, \\
therefore 8x-24+x=12, \\
therefore x=\boxed{4}, \\
therefore AD=4.

答案文本展示了至少 4 步以上的中间推导（设元、求 AF/CF/CE/BE/BD、最后用 AD+BD=AB 求 x）。

normalization_record.normalized_question_text: As shown in the diagram, the equilateral triangle $\triangle ABC$ has side length $12$. Point $D$ moves on $AB$. Through $D$, draw $DE \perp BC$ at $E$; through $E$, draw $EF \perp AC$ at $F$; through $F$, draw $FG \perp AB$ at $G$. When $G$ coincides with $D$, what is the length of $AD$?
normalization_record.normalized_answer_text: Let $AD=x$. Since $\triangle ABC$ is equilateral, $\angle A=\angle B=\angle C=60^\circ$. Also, $DE\perp BC$ at $E$, $EF\perp AC$ at $F$, and $FG\perp AB$ at $G$, so $\angle BDF=\angle DEB=\angle EFC=90^\circ$. Therefore $AF=2x$, so $CF=12-2x$. Then $CE=2CF=24-4x$, hence $BE=12-CE=4x-12$, and $BD=2BE=8x-24$. Since $AD+BD=AB$, we have $x+(8x-24)=12$, so $x=\boxed{4}$. Therefore, $AD=4$.

open_ended_problem_variants.expected_answer: Let $AD=x$. Since $\triangle ABC$ is equilateral, $\angle A=\angle B=\angle C=60^\circ$. Also, $DE\perp BC$ at $E$, $EF\perp AC$ at $F$, and $FG\perp AB$ at $G$, so $\angle BDF=\angle DEB=\angle EFC=90^\circ$. Therefore $AF=2x$, so $CF=12-2x$. Then $CE=2CF=24-4x$, hence $BE=12-CE=4x-12$, and $BD=2BE=8x-24$. Since $AD+BD=AB$, we have $x+(8x-24)=12$, so $x=\boxed{4}$. Therefore, $AD=4$.
